raw.population: Sustained Release VPA ADULTS N:121 Sex: m=65, f=56 Age: 22 ±8.8 CHILDREN N: 112 Sex: m=53, f=59 Age: 9.15 ±3.12 Enteric Coated VPA ADULTS N:89 Sex: m=40, f=49 Age: 24.1 ±8.5 CHILDREN N: 110 Sex: m=52, f=58 Age: 9.53 ±3.1 VPA – monotherapy VERY YOUNG PATIENT N: 42 Sex: m=23, f=19 Age: 1.21 ±0.54
raw.objective: “To evaluate the ability of therapeutic drug monitoring (TDM) using model-based, goal-oriented Bayesian adaptive control for help in planning, monitoring, and adjusting individualized VAL dosing regimens.”
raw.observations: The model, based on only central tendencies, is not a good predictor of inter-individual variability in patient response. But allows for VAL dosage prediction and adjustment without having to wait for steady-state. High average age in the EC group may explain more ‘adult-like’ distribution of volume in child group. No clear evidence of any nonlinear increase in VAL clearance with increasing daily dose in our adult populations. Also the model had no tendency to overestimate or underestimate the measured total concentrations of VAL found after increases in dosage. The simple model does not consider absorptive time delay of EC tablet, and the apparent increase in CL for total VPA at high doses. The observed nonlinear CL to DDVPA relationship may be explained by higher doses prescribed in clinical practice to individuals with higher drug CL.
raw.dose: Steady state, oral doses in patients
raw.structure: one-compartment model with first-order absorption and elimination
raw.equations: --- EC --- ADULT Kel= theta1*exp(eta1) V= (theta2*WT)*exp(eta2) CL=Kel * V KA= theta3*exp(eta3) CHILD Kel= theta4*exp(eta4) V= (theta5*WT)*exp(eta5) CL=Kel * V KA=theta6*exp(eta6) --- SR --- ADULT Kel= theta7*exp(eta7) V= (theta8*WT)*exp(eta8) CL=Kel * V KA=theta9*exp(eta9) CHILD Kel= theta10*exp(eta10) V= (theta11*WT)*exp(eta11) CL=Kel * V KA=theta12*exp(eta12) --- MONO --- INFANT Kel= theta13*exp(eta13) V= (theta14*WT)*exp(eta14) CL=Kel * V KA=theta15*exp(eta15) #no error model reported Cobs=Cipred

normalization.omega: Values labelled OMEGA in the appendix are retained as NONMEM variances; no CV reconversion is applied.
normalization.eta: ETA distribution is inferred only when the appendix equation explicitly uses exp(ETA) or additive ETA.
normalization.source_text: The appendix row is preserved verbatim in raw; normalized fields never replace it.
